title: Goeppertia peregrina

intro: Goeppertia peregrina, also known as Peregrina Calathea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Goeppertia peregrina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
